step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that immediate cesarean section (Option C) should be performed as it addresses both the obstruction and the need for rapid removal of the deceased fetus while allowing proper hydration management afterward. However, craniotomy (Option A), which involves surgically removing part of an infant's skull before delivering the rest of its body, would not only complicate the procedure but may lead to significant neonatal morbidity even though there has been fetal demise due to lack of viability. Therefore, Option C remains the best course of action considering all factors involved.
